8	/-!
9	# Grassmannian affine charts
10	
11	This file contains the single blueprint-pinned declaration for the affine
12	charts of the Grassmannian scheme: `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.affineChart`.
13	
14	For `I ⊆ Fin r` with `#I = d`, the affine chart `U^I` of the Grassmannian
15	`Gr(d, r)` is the spectrum of the polynomial ring in the `d(r-d)` free matrix
16	entries — `Spec ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]_{q ∉ I}` — which is non-canonically isomorphic
17	to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.
18	
19	Blueprint reference: `def:gr_affine_chart`
20	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`).
21	-/
22	
23	set_option autoImplicit false
24	
25	universe u
26	
27	open AlgebraicGeometry
28	
29	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
30	
31	/- Blueprint: def:gr_affine_chart (chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex) -/
32	
33	/- Planner note:
34	   Blueprint `def:gr_affine_chart` (Nitsure §1, "Construction by gluing together
35	   affine patches"): for `I ⊆ {1..r}` with `#I = d`, `X^I` is the `d×r` matrix
36	   whose `I`-minor is the `d×d` identity and whose other `d(r-d)` entries are
37	   independent indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` over ℤ. The affine chart is
38	   `U^I := Spec ℤ[X^I] = Spec (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)` —
39	   the spectrum of the polynomial ring on the `d(r-d)` free entries; non-canonically
40	   `≅ 𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`. The prover should build `affineChart` as
41	   `AlgebraicGeometry.Spec` of that `CommRingCat` (the MvPolynomial ring on the
42	   free-entry index type), via
43	   `MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` or an equivalent index of cardinality
44	   `d(r-d)`. -/
45	
46	/-- The **affine chart** `U^I` of the Grassmannian `Gr(d, r)` indexed by a
47	`d`-element subset `I : Finset (Fin r)`.
48	
49	Concretely `U^I = Spec ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]_{p : Fin d, q ∉ I}`, the spectrum of the
50	polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ` in the `d(r-d)`
51	free entries of the standard matrix representative with `I`-minor equal to the
52	identity. This chart is non-canonically isomorphic to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.
53	
54	The Grassmannian scheme is obtained by gluing the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts
55	`U^I` along the Plücker cocycle transition maps. -/
56	noncomputable def affineChart (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) : Scheme :=
57	  AlgebraicGeometry.Spec (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ))
58	
59	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Grassmannian transition maps
60	
61	The transition map `θ_{I,J}` of `def:gr_transition` is a Cramer-inverse computation
62	over the localised chart ring `R^I_J := ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`, followed by the universal
63	property of the away-localisation. We build it bottom-up over the chart ring
64	`R^I := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ`, all elementary matrix algebra
65	over `ℤ`. The `d`-subset combinatorics are handled by the order isomorphism
66	`Finset.orderIsoOfFin : I.card = d → (Fin d ≃o ↥I)`.
67	
68	Blueprint references: `def:gr_universal_matrix`, `def:gr_minor_det`,
69	`def:gr_universal_minor`, `lem:gr_minorDet_unit`, `def:gr_universalMinorInv`,
70	`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`, `def:gr_image_matrix`, `def:gr_transition_pre`
71	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
72	
73	/-- The **universal matrix** `X^I` (`def:gr_universal_matrix`): the `d × r` matrix
74	over the chart ring `R^I = MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` whose `I`-minor is the
75	`d × d` identity (read through the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥I`) and whose remaining
76	`d(r-d)` entries are the free indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` (`q ∉ I`). Project-local:
77	the universal point of the affine chart `affineChart d r I`. -/
78	noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
79	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
80	  fun p q =>
81	    if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
82	    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)
83	
84	/-- The **minor determinant** `P^I_J = det(X^I_J)` (`def:gr_minor_det`): the determinant
85	of the `d × d` submatrix of `universalMatrix d r I` whose columns are those indexed by
86	`J`, reindexed to `Fin d` via the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥J`. Project-local: defines the
87	principal open `U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]`. -/
88	noncomputable def minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
89	    (hJ : J.card = d) : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
90	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
91	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
92	
93	/-- The **localised `J`-minor** `X^I_J` over `R^I_J` (`def:gr_universal_minor`): the
94	`J`-minor of `universalMatrix d r I` with entries pushed forward along the structure map
95	`R^I → R^I_J = Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`. Project-local. -/
96	noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
97	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
98	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
99	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
100	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map (algebraMap _ _)
101	
102	/-- The localised minor determinant is a unit (`lem:gr_minorDet_unit`): `det(X^I_J)` is the
103	image of `P^I_J` under the away-localisation structure map, hence a unit. Project-local. -/
104	theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
105	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
106	  have h : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
107	      = (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
108	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
109	  rw [h]
110	  exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
111	
112	/-- The **Cramer inverse** `(X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_universalMinorInv`): the Mathlib
113	nonsingular inverse of the localised `J`-minor. Its entries lie in `R^I_J` by Cramer's
114	rule. Project-local. -/
115	noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
116	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
117	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
118	  (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹
119	
120	/-- The Cramer inverse is a two-sided inverse (`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`):
121	since `det(X^I_J)` is a unit, `(X^I_J)⁻¹` is a genuine left and right inverse.
122	Project-local. -/
123	theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
124	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
125	    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
126	    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
127	  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
128	   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩
129	
130	/-- The **image matrix** `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`def:gr_image_matrix`): the product of the
131	Cramer inverse with the universal matrix base-changed to `R^I_J`. Its entries are the
132	prospective images of the indeterminates `x^J_{p,q}` under `θ_{I,J}`. Project-local. -/
133	noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
134	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
135	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
136	  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)
137	
138	/-- The **pre-localisation hom** `θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (`def:gr_transition_pre`): the
139	`ℤ`-algebra map out of the chart ring of `J` sending each free indeterminate `x^J_{p,q}`
140	to the `(p,q)`-entry of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
141	noncomputable def transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
142	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
143	    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ →ₐ[ℤ]
144	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
145	  MvPolynomial.aeval (fun e => imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ e.1 e.2.1)
146	
147	/-- The `I`-minor of the universal matrix `X^I` is the `d × d` identity (`P^I_I = 1`
148	underlies `lem:gr_transition_self`): the `I`-columns of `X^I` read through the order iso
149	`Fin d ≃o ↥I` form the identity block. Project-local. -/
150	theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
151	    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
152	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
153	  ext p p'
154	  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
155	  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
156	  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
157	    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
158	  by_cases h : p = p'
159	  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
160	  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]
161	
162	/-- Submatrix on the columns of a right matrix factor commutes with the product
163	(rows reindexed by `id`). Project-local helper: avoids a matrix-multiplication
164	instance-keying issue that blocks the generic `rw` of Mathlib's submatrix-mul lemmas. -/
165	private lemma mul_submatrix_col {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
166	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
167	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
168	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
169	
170	/-- The `J`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the identity: `M_J =
171	(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_J = 1` (`def:gr_image_matrix`, used in the cocycle/transition arguments).
172	Project-local. -/
173	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
174	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
175	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
176	      (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
177	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
178	        (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))
179	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
180	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
181	          (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
182	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map]
183	  exact (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
184	
185	/-- The `I`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the Cramer inverse:
186	`M_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
187	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_I (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
188	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
189	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
190	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ := by
191	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
192	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
193	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
194	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
195	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
196	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
197	    Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _), mul_one]
198	
199	/-- The pre-localisation hom realises the matrix formula `θ̃_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`:
200	applying `θ̃_{I,J}` entrywise to the universal matrix `X^J` yields the image matrix `M`
201	(`def:gr_transition_pre`, `def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
202	theorem universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
203	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
204	    (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)
205	      = imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ := by
206	  ext p q
207	  simp only [Matrix.map_apply, universalMatrix]
208	  by_cases hq : q ∈ J
209	  · rw [dif_pos hq]
210	    set k := (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ).symm ⟨q, hq⟩ with hk
211	    have hqk : (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k : Fin r) = q := by simp [hk]
212	    have himg : imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ p q = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) _) p k := by
213	      have e := congrFun (congrFun (imageMatrix_submatrix_self d r I J hI hJ) p) k
214	      rw [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq] at e
215	      rw [← hqk]; exact e
216	    rw [himg, Matrix.one_apply, apply_ite (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ), map_one, map_zero]
217	    have hcond : ((J.orderIsoOfFin hJ p : Fin r) = q) ↔ (p = k) := by
218	      conv_lhs => rw [← hqk]
219	      rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
220	    by_cases hpk : p = k
221	    · rw [if_pos (hcond.mpr hpk), if_pos hpk]
222	    · rw [if_neg (hcond.not.mpr hpk), if_neg hpk]
223	  · rw [dif_neg hq, transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X]
224	
225	/-- The pre-hom sends `P^J_I` to a unit (`lem:gr_transition_pre_unit`):
226	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I) = det((X^I_J)⁻¹) = det(X^I_J)⁻¹ = 1/P^I_J`, a unit of `R^I_J`. This is the
227	hypothesis that lets `θ̃_{I,J}` extend along the away-localisation `R^J[1/P^J_I]`.
228	Project-local. -/
229	theorem isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
230	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)) := by
231	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
232	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
233	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
234	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
235	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
236	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
237	  have hmul : (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).det * (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det = 1 := by
238	    rw [← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]
239	  exact IsUnit.of_mul_eq_one _ hmul
240	
241	/-- The **transition map** `θ_{I,J} : R^J[1/P^J_I] → R^I[1/P^I_J]` (`def:gr_transition`):
242	the away-localisation lift of the pre-hom `θ̃_{I,J}` along `P^J_I`, available because
243	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I)` is a unit (`isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet`). It is the comorphism of
244	the chart-overlap isomorphism `U^I_J → U^J_I`. Project-local. -/
245	noncomputable def transitionMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
246	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
247	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) →+* Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
248	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
249	    (g := (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
250	    (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J hI hJ)
251	
252	/-- `θ_{I,I}` is the identity (`lem:gr_transition_self`): since `P^I_I = 1` the minor
253	`X^I_I = 1` is its own Cramer inverse, so the pre-hom is the structure map and its
254	away-localisation lift is the identity ring homomorphism. Project-local. -/
255	theorem transitionMap_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
256	    transitionMap d r I I hI hI = RingHom.id (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) := by
257	  have hmin1 : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
258	    simp only [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self]
259	    exact Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)
260	  have hinv1 : universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
261	    simp only [universalMinorInv, hmin1]
262	    exact inv_one
263	  have himg : imageMatrix d r I I hI hI = (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _) := by
264	    rw [imageMatrix, hinv1]
265	    exact Matrix.one_mul _
266	  have hpre : (transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI).toRingHom
267	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
268	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) := by
269	    apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
270	    · intro n
271	      change transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI (MvPolynomial.C n) = algebraMap _ _ (MvPolynomial.C n)
272	      rw [transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_C,
273	        IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply ℤ (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
274	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)), MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
275	    · intro e
276	      change transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI (MvPolynomial.X e) = algebraMap _ _ (MvPolynomial.X e)
277	      rw [transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, himg, Matrix.map_apply]
278	      congr 1
279	      rw [universalMatrix, dif_neg e.2.2]
280	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r I I hI hI))
281	  simp only [transitionMap, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, RingHom.id_comp]
282	  exact hpre
283	
284	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — triple-overlap rings and the cocycle
285	
286	The cocycle condition `θ_{I,K} = θ_{I,J} ∘ θ_{J,K}` cannot be stated as a naive
287	composition of the landed `transitionMap`s: the codomain of `transitionMap d r J K`
288	(`R^J[1/P^J_K]`) differs from the domain of `transitionMap d r I J` (`R^J[1/P^J_I]`).
289	The identity therefore lives over the *triple-overlap* rings obtained by inverting BOTH
290	relevant minors in each chart ring:
291	`S_K := R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)]`, `S_J := R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]`, `S_I := R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`.
292	We build the localised transition maps `Θ_{I,J} : S_J →+* S_I`, `Θ_{J,K} : S_K →+* S_J`,
293	`Θ_{I,K} : S_K →+* S_I` over these rings and verify the cocycle.
294	
295	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_cocycle` (`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
296	
297	/-- A ring homomorphism carries the nonsingular (Cramer) inverse to the nonsingular
298	inverse, provided the determinant is a unit. Project-local helper: Mathlib has no direct
299	`map`-`nonsingular inverse` compatibility lemma. -/
300	private lemma map_nonsing_inv {n : ℕ} {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
301	    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
302	    (A.map f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map f := by
303	  have hmul : (A.map f) * (A⁻¹.map f) = 1 := by
304	    rw [← Matrix.map_mul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A h, Matrix.map_one f (map_zero f) (map_one f)]
305	  exact Matrix.inv_eq_right_inv hmul
306	
307	/-- Inclusion of the away-localisation at `x` into the away-localisation at `x * y`
308	(inverting the extra factor `y`). Project-local: the structure map of the triple overlap
309	relative to a double-localisation. -/
310	noncomputable def awayInclLeft {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
311	    Localization.Away x →+* Localization.Away (x * y) :=
312	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := Localization.Away x) x
313	    (g := algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)))
314	    (by
315	      have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
316	        IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
317	      rw [map_mul] at h
318	      exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h)
319	
320	/-- Inclusion of the away-localisation at `y` into the away-localisation at `x * y`
321	(inverting the extra factor `x`). Project-local. -/
322	noncomputable def awayInclRight {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
323	    Localization.Away y →+* Localization.Away (x * y) :=
324	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := Localization.Away y) y
325	    (g := algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)))
326	    (by
327	      have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
328	        IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
329	      rw [map_mul] at h
330	      exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_right h)
331	
332	/-- `awayInclLeft` is the canonical map over `R`: it intertwines the two structure maps.
333	Project-local. -/
334	lemma awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
335	    (awayInclLeft x y).comp (algebraMap R (Localization.Away x)) =
336	      algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) := by
337	  rw [awayInclLeft]; exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp x _
338	
339	/-- `awayInclRight` is the canonical map over `R`: it intertwines the two structure maps.
340	Project-local. -/
341	lemma awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
342	    (awayInclRight x y).comp (algebraMap R (Localization.Away y)) =
343	      algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) := by
344	  rw [awayInclRight]; exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp y _
345	
346	/-- The image of a general minor determinant `P^B_C` under the pre-localisation hom
347	`θ̃_{A,B}` is the determinant of the `C`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^A_B)⁻¹ X^A`
348	(generalises the `C = A` computation underlying `lem:gr_transition_pre_unit`). Project-local. -/
349	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
350	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
351	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
352	      = ((imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
353	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).det := by
354	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
355	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
356	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).map
357	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
358	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
359	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap]
360	
361	/-- The "cross" minor `P^B_C` is sent by `θ̃_{A,B}`, then pushed into a double localisation
362	`D` (in which `P^A_C` is a unit), to a unit. Concretely
363	`θ̃_{A,B}(P^B_C) = det((X^A_B)⁻¹ X^A_C) = det((X^A_B)⁻¹) · P^A_C`, a product of two units
364	once `P^A_C` is inverted. This is the cross-factor input to each triple-overlap transition
365	lift. Project-local. -/
366	private lemma isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross
367	    (d r : ℕ) (A B C : Finset (Fin r)) (hA : A.card = d) (hB : B.card = d) (hC : C.card = d)
368	    {D : Type*} [CommRing D] [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D]
369	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB) →+* D)
370	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ)
371	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)))
372	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D)
373	    (hunit : IsUnit (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D
374	        (minorDet d r A C hA hC))) :
375	    IsUnit (incl (transitionPreMap d r A B hA hB (minorDet d r B C hB hC))) := by
376	  have hsub : (imageMatrix d r A B hA hB).submatrix id
377	        (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))
378	      = universalMinorInv d r A B hA hB *
379	        (((universalMatrix d r A hA).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
380	          (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
381	  rw [transitionPreMap_minorDet, hsub, Matrix.det_mul, map_mul]
382	  refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
383	  · refine IsUnit.map incl ?_
384	    refine IsUnit.of_mul_eq_one (universalMinor d r A B hA hB).det ?_
385	    rw [← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r A B hA hB).1, Matrix.det_one]
386	  · have hdet : (((universalMatrix d r A hA).map
387	            (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)))).submatrix id
388	            (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))).det
389	          = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)) (minorDet d r A C hA hC) := by
390	      rw [Matrix.submatrix_map]
391	      exact (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
392	    rw [hdet]
393	    have hcomp : incl (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ)
394	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)) (minorDet d r A C hA hC))
395	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D (minorDet d r A C hA hC) :=
396	      RingHom.congr_fun hincl _
397	    rw [hcomp]
398	    exact hunit
399	
400	/-- The left factor of a product is a unit in the away-localisation at that product.
401	Project-local. -/
402	private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_away_left {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
403	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) x) := by
404	  have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
405	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
406	  rw [map_mul] at h
407	  exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h
408	
409	/-- The right factor of a product is a unit in the away-localisation at that product.
410	Project-local. -/
411	private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_away_right {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
412	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) y) := by
413	  have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
414	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
415	  rw [map_mul] at h
416	  exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_right h
417	
418	/-- The triple-overlap transition map `Θ_{I,J} : S_J →+* S_I`, where
419	`S_J = R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]` and `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`: the away-localisation lift of
420	`θ̃_{I,J}` (post-composed into `S_I`) along the doubly-inverted minor `P^J_I P^J_K`.
421	Project-local. -/
422	noncomputable def cocycleΘIJ (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
423	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
424	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI * minorDet d r J K hJ hK) →+*
425	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK) :=
426	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r J I hJ hI * minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
427	    (g := (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
428	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
429	    (by
430	      rw [map_mul]
431	      refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
432	      · exact (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J hI hJ).map
433	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
434	      · exact isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross d r I J K hI hJ hK
435	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
436	          (awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap _ _)
437	          (isUnit_algebraMap_away_right _ _))
438	
439	/-- The triple-overlap transition map `Θ_{J,K} : S_K →+* S_J`, where
440	`S_K = R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)]` and `S_J = R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]`. Project-local. -/

88	/-! ## §1. Quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_X`-algebras
89	
90	For a scheme `X`, a quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras is a sheaf of
91	`O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent. Stacks tag
92	01LL packages the notion as a sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ on $X$ taking values in
93	commutative rings together with a unit map from the structure sheaf
94	$\mathcal{O}_X$, plus the quasi-coherence requirement.
95	
96	iter-179 (Block A) packages this as a triple of (i) a sheaf of CommRings,
97	(ii) an `O_X`-algebra unit from the structure sheaf, and (iii) the
98	`Coequifibered` overlay (Stacks 01LL form): the affine restriction of the
99	unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. on every affine open `U` and section
100	`f`, the restriction-to-basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
101	This is the strictly-weaker, sheafified-tensor-free formulation that
102	Mathlib's `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes
103	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); it is
104	equivalent to the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` predicate under the
105	dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. See
106	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the iter-178 consult that
107	identified this idiom.
108	
109	Blueprint reference: `def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras` (Stacks 01LL,
110	situation-relative-spec). -/
111	
112	/-- A **quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras** (iter-179 Block A,
113	Mathlib-aligned form).
114	
115	A triple of
116	- `sheaf` : a sheaf of commutative rings on the underlying topological space
117	  of `X`,
118	- `unit` : an `O_X`-algebra unit, i.e. a morphism of sheaves of commutative
119	  rings `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` from the structure sheaf to the carrier, and
120	- `coequifibered` : the Stacks-01LL form of quasi-coherence — every
121	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is the
122	  `IsLocalization.Away f` of `sheaf U`. This is the exact predicate
123	  consumed by `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
124	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
125	
126	The shape matches the input of Mathlib's relative-gluing construction
127	verbatim, so `RelativeSpec` is defined directly as
128	`(relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`. The
129	`NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly weaker than the full
130	sheaf-of-modules quasi-coherence predicate
131	`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent`, but equivalent under the dense-subsite
132	equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`, so no information is lost. See
133	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2 for the consult that
134	identified this carrier shape. -/
135	structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where
136	  /-- The underlying sheaf of commutative rings on `X`. -/
137	  sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace

154	/-! ## §2. The relative-spectrum scheme
155	
156	The construction proceeds affine-locally: on an affine open `U = Spec R ⊆ X` we
157	set `π⁻¹(U) := Spec(𝒜(U))`, where `𝒜(U)` is regarded as an `R`-algebra. The local
158	pieces glue compatibly because `𝒜` is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
159	`𝒜(U) ⊗_R S ≅ 𝒜(V)` for `V = Spec S ⊆ U` gives an open immersion of the
160	corresponding Specs).
161	
162	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_exists` (Stacks 01LQ
163	lemma-glue-relative-spec). -/
164	
165	end RelativeSpec
166	
167	/-- The **relative spectrum** scheme `Spec_X(𝒜)` of a quasi-coherent sheaf of
168	`O_X`-algebras `𝒜`.
169	
170	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
171	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`
172	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:102`,
173	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`). The
174	`coequifibered` field of `QcohAlgebra` is precisely the Stacks-01LL form of
175	quasi-coherence that Mathlib's `relativeGluingData` consumes: the affine
176	restriction of the `O_X`-algebra unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. every
177	basic-open restriction is an `IsLocalization.Away`. The construction glues the
178	affine pieces `Spec(𝒜(U))` along the directed affine open cover
179	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover X` and is the exact Mathlib-aligned
180	template used in `Hom.normalization`
181	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:120`); see
182	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the consult that identified this
183	idiom. -/
184	noncomputable def RelativeSpec {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : Scheme.{u} :=
185	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued
186	
187	/-- The **structure morphism** `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum.
188	
189	This declaration is needed to express the intended substantive type of
190	`UniversalProperty`, which references the structure morphism.
191	
192	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
193	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase`
194	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:114`). The map is the colimit
195	descent of the natural transformation `Spec(𝒜(U)) → U` over the directed
196	affine open cover; see `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 3.
197	
198	Blueprint reference: implicit in `thm:relative_spec_exists`. -/
199	noncomputable def RelativeSpec.structureMorphism {X : Scheme.{u}}
200	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : X.RelativeSpec 𝒜 ⟶ X :=
201	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase

205	/-! ## §3. Universal property — affine structure morphism
206	
207	The Stacks 01LQ universal property says `Spec_X(𝒜)` represents the functor
208	sending an `X`-scheme `g : T → X` to the set of `O_X`-algebra maps
209	`𝒜 → g_* O_T`. A direct structural consequence of representability is that the
210	structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is *affine* (Stacks 01LR
211	lemma-spec-properties, immediate corollary). For the iter-173 file-skeleton we
212	encode the universal property by this affine-morphism consequence — the
213	substantive content is the same up to body unfolding, and the type is
214	non-tautological.
215	
216	iter-174+: refine the signature to a `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy`
217	witness against the functor of `O_X`-algebra maps once `QcohAlgebra` is
218	unpacked and the Hom-set on algebras is available.
219	
220	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_univ` (Stacks 01LQ lemma-spec). -/
221	
222	/-- **Universal property of the relative spectrum (affine-structure form).**
223	
224	The structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum is affine.
225	This is the substantive consequence of the Stacks 01LQ representability
226	statement (an `X`-scheme is the relative spectrum of some quasi-coherent
227	algebra iff its structure morphism is affine).
228	
229	iter-174+: refine the type signature to the full Yoneda-bijection statement
230	`Hom_X(T, Spec_X(𝒜)) ≃ Hom_{O_X-alg}(𝒜, g_* O_T)` once `QcohAlgebra` is
231	unpacked and an `O_X`-algebra Hom-set is in scope. The current type is the
232	non-tautological structural consequence used downstream by `affine_base_iff`. -/
233	theorem UniversalProperty {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
234	    IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := by
235	  -- Mathlib `relativeGluingData` builder; per `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
236	  -- Block B. We invoke `isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`: for each `x : X`
237	  -- pick an affine open `U ∋ x` (every `X` has such by `exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`),
238	  -- and identify the structure-morphism preimage of `U` with the range of the colimit
239	  -- inclusion of the affine fiber `Spec(𝒜(U))` (via
240	  -- `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`); affineness of the
241	  -- opens-range then follows from `isAffineOpen_opensRange` since the source is
242	  -- `Scheme.Spec` of `𝒜.sheaf.val.obj (op U)`, an affine scheme.
243	  apply isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen
244	  intro x
245	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hxU, _⟩ :=
246	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (U := ⊤) (Set.mem_univ x)
247	  refine ⟨U, hxU, hU, ?_⟩
248	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered
249	  let i : X.AffineZariskiSite := ⟨U, hU⟩
250	  change IsAffineOpen (d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U)
251	  have key : d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U = (d.cover.f i).opensRange := by
252	    have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι i
253	    simpa [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
254	  rw [key]
255	  have : IsAffine (d.cover.X i) := by
256	    change IsAffine (Scheme.Spec.obj _)
257	    infer_instance
258	  exact isAffineOpen_opensRange _
259	
260	/-! ## §4. Affine base case
261	
262	When the base `X = Spec R` is affine, `Spec_X(𝒜)` reduces to the absolute
263	spectrum of the global sections: `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`. The substantive
264	content for the iter-173 scaffold is that the relative spectrum is itself
265	affine. This is Stacks 01LO lemma-spec-affine.
266	
267	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_affine_base`. -/
268	
269	/-- **Affine-base reduction of the relative spectrum.**
270	
271	For an affine scheme `X = Spec R` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`,
272	the relative spectrum `Spec_X(𝒜)` is itself an affine scheme. (More precisely,
273	there is a canonical isomorphism `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`, but extracting
274	`Γ(X, 𝒜) : CommRingCat` requires the unpacked structure of `QcohAlgebra`,
275	which is iter-174+ work.)
276	
277	iter-174+: refine to the full statement
278	`Nonempty ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜 ≅ Spec (Γ((Spec R), 𝒜)))`
279	once `Γ` for `QcohAlgebra` is in scope. -/
280	theorem affine_base_iff {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (𝒜 : (Spec R).QcohAlgebra) :
281	    IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜) := by
282	  -- Affineness of the relative-spec total space when the base is affine is the
283	  -- standard "affine over affine is affine" consequence: by
284	  -- `UniversalProperty` the structure morphism is `IsAffineHom`, and `Spec R`
285	  -- is itself `IsAffine`, so `isAffine_of_isAffineHom` closes the goal.
286	  have h : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := UniversalProperty 𝒜
287	  exact isAffine_of_isAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
288	
289	end RelativeSpec

975	theorem gf_generic_rank_ses
976	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
977	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N]
978	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
979	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
980	    ∃ (m : ℕ) (φ : (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
981	        →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin d) A] N),
982	      Function.Injective φ ∧
983	      Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) := by
984	  classical
985	  -- Notation: `P = A[X_1,…,X_d]`, its non-zero divisors `S`, fraction field `K`,
986	  -- localisation `NK = N_Q`, and the localisation map `ℓ : N → NK`.
987	  let P := MvPolynomial (Fin d) A
988	  let S := nonZeroDivisors P
989	  let K := Localization S
990	  let NK := LocalizedModule S N
991	  let ℓ : N →ₗ[P] NK := LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S N
992	  -- `NK` is a finite-dimensional `K`-vector space; take a basis indexed by `Fin m`.
993	  let m := Module.finrank K NK

995	  -- Lift each basis vector along `ℓ`, clearing its denominator.
996	  obtain ⟨lift, hlift⟩ :
997	      ∃ lift : Fin m → N × S, ∀ i, ((lift i).2 : P) • b i = ℓ (lift i).1 := by
998	    refine ⟨fun i => (IsLocalizedModule.surj S ℓ (b i)).choose, fun i => ?_⟩

1001	  -- The denominators map to units of `K`.

1056	      refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
1057	      rw [map_smul]
1058	      -- `(s:P) • (c i • ℓ v_i) = a i • ℓ v_i`
1059	      rw [← algebraMap_smul K ((s : P)) (c i • ℓ (v i)), smul_smul, ← Algebra.smul_def,
1060	        ← hae i, algebraMap_smul]
1061	    -- So `s • n - φ a` lies in the kernel of `ℓ`, hence is killed by some `t ∈ S`.
1062	    have hker : ℓ ((s : P) • n - Fintype.linearCombination P v a) = 0 := by
1063	      rw [map_sub, key, sub_self]
1064	    obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := (IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S ℓ).mp hker
1065	    -- The non-zero divisor `t * s` annihilates the class of `n` in `T`.
1066	    refine ⟨t * s, ?_⟩
1067	    rw [smul_sub, sub_eq_zero] at ht
1068	    have ht2 : (↑t : P) • ((↑s : P) • n) = (↑t : P) • Fintype.linearCombination P v a := ht
1069	    have hmem : (↑(t * s) : P) • n ∈ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination P v) := by
1070	      rw [Submonoid.coe_mul, mul_smul, ht2, ← map_smul]
1071	      exact LinearMap.mem_range_self _ _
1072	    -- Conclude `(t * s) • ⟦n⟧ = 0`.
1073	    rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul, Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero]
1074	    exact hmem
1075	
1076	/-- **L5b.1 — annihilator extraction for the torsion module**
1077	(`lem:gf_torsion_annihilator`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a noetherian domain, `d ≥ 0`,
1078	and `T` a finite *torsion* module over `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exists a
1079	non-zero `F ∈ Ann_{P_d}(T)`. Since `P_d` is a domain, a non-zero-divisor
1080	annihilator (provided by `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`) is the
1081	same thing as a non-zero one. -/
1082	theorem gf_torsion_annihilator
1083	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A] (d : ℕ)
1084	    (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
1085	    [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
1086	    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
1087	    ∃ F : MvPolynomial (Fin d) A, F ≠ 0 ∧
1088	      F ∈ Module.annihilator (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T := by
1089	  obtain ⟨F, hFann, hFnzd⟩ := Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors htors
1090	  refine ⟨F, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hFnzd, ?_⟩
1091	  rw [← Submodule.annihilator_top]
1092	  exact hFann
1093	
1094	/-! ### L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables (`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`)
1095	
1096	The single-variable elimination of the support-dimension drop needs a triangular
1097	change of variables `e` making a non-zero `F ∈ A[X_0,…,X_n]` monic in `X_0` up to a
1098	unit after inverting one `g ∈ A`. Mathlib's Noether-normalisation development
1099	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization`) constructs exactly this transformation
1100	`T` and proves the leading coefficient is a unit — but only over a *field*, and as
1101	`private` lemmas. The block below replays that construction over a noetherian *domain*
1102	(the field is used in Mathlib only to conclude the top coefficient is a unit; over a
1103	domain it is merely non-zero, and becomes a unit after inverting it). The degree
1104	bookkeeping (`degreeOf_zero_t`, `leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t`, `T_leadingcoeff_eq`) is
1105	a domain-adapted transcription of the Mathlib originals. -/
1106	
1107	section NagataNormalization
1108	
1109	-- `AlgebraicGeometry.Polynomial` exists and would shadow the root namespace, so the
1110	-- `Polynomial`/`MvPolynomial` lemma APIs are opened with explicit `_root_.` prefixes.
1111	open _root_.Polynomial _root_.MvPolynomial _root_.Ideal Nat _root_.RingHom List
1112	
1113	/-- `finSuccEquiv` commutes with coefficient base change `MvPolynomial.map phi`. -/
1114	private theorem finSuccEquiv_map_comm {A B : Type*} [CommRing A] [CommRing B]
1115	    (phi : A →+* B) (m : ℕ) (q : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) :
1116	    finSuccEquiv B m (MvPolynomial.map phi q)
1117	      = Polynomial.map (MvPolynomial.map phi) (finSuccEquiv A m q) := by
1118	  induction q using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1119	  | C a => simp [finSuccEquiv_apply]
1120	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
1121	  | mul_X p i hp =>
1122	    simp only [map_mul, Polynomial.map_mul, hp]
1123	    congr 1
1124	    cases i using Fin.cases with
1125	    | zero => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_zero]
1126	    | succ j => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ]
1127	
1128	variable {k : Type*} [CommRing k] [IsDomain k] {n : ℕ} (f : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k)
1129	variable (v w : Fin (n + 1) →₀ ℕ)
1130	
1131	local notation3 "up" => 2 + f.totalDegree
1132	local notation3 "r" => fun (i : Fin (n + 1)) ↦ up ^ i.1
1133	
1134	omit [IsDomain k] in
1135	variable {f v} in
1136	private lemma lt_up (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) : ∀ l ∈ ofFn v, l < up := by grind
1137	
1138	/-- The triangular Nagata substitution `X_i ↦ X_i + c • X_0 ^ (up ^ i)` (`i ≠ 0`),
1139	`X_0 ↦ X_0`. Transcribed from `Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization` to a domain. -/
1140	private noncomputable abbrev T1 (c : k) :
1141	    MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k →ₐ[k] MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k :=
1142	  aeval fun i ↦ if i = 0 then X 0 else X i + c • X 0 ^ r i
1143	
1144	omit [IsDomain k] in
1145	private lemma t1_comp_t1_neg (c : k) : (T1 f c).comp (T1 f (-c)) = AlgHom.id _ _ := by
1146	  rw [comp_aeval, ← MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]; ext i v; cases i using Fin.cases <;> simp
1147	
1148	/-- The Nagata transformation as an `A`-algebra automorphism. -/
1149	private noncomputable abbrev T := AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom (T1 f 1) (T1 f (-1))
1150	  (t1_comp_t1_neg f 1) (by simpa using t1_comp_t1_neg f (-1))
1151	
1152	omit [IsDomain k] in
1153	private lemma sum_r_mul_ne (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) (wlt : ∀ i, w i < up) (ne : v ≠ w) :
1154	    ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * v x ≠ ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * w x := by
1155	  intro h
1156	  refine ne <| Finsupp.ext <| congrFun <| ofFn_inj.mp ?_
1157	  apply ofDigits_inj_of_len_eq (Nat.lt_add_right f.totalDegree one_lt_two)
1158	    (by simp) (lt_up vlt) (lt_up wlt)
1159	  simpa only [ofDigits_eq_sum_mapIdx, mapIdx_eq_ofFn, get_ofFn, length_ofFn,
1160	    Fin.val_cast, mul_comm, sum_ofFn] using h
1161	
1162	private lemma degreeOf_zero_t {a : k} (ha : a ≠ 0) : ((T f) (monomial v a)).degreeOf 0 =
1163	    ∑ i : Fin (n + 1), (r i) * v i := by
1164	  rw [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv, monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_pow v fun a ↦ X a]
1165	  simp only [Fin.prod_univ_succ, Fin.sum_univ_succ, map_mul, map_prod, map_pow,
1166	    AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, MvPolynomial.aeval_C, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, if_pos, Fin.succ_ne_zero,
1167	    ite_false, one_smul, map_add, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, finSuccEquiv_X_succ, algebraMap_eq]
1168	  have h (i : Fin n) :
1169	      (Polynomial.C (X (R := k) i) + Polynomial.X ^ r i.succ) ^ v i.succ ≠ 0 :=
1170	    pow_ne_zero (v i.succ) (leadingCoeff_ne_zero.mp <| by simp [add_comm, leadingCoeff_X_pow_add_C])
1171	  rw [natDegree_mul (by simp [ha]) (mul_ne_zero (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr
1172	    (fun i _ ↦ h i))), natDegree_mul (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr (fun i _ ↦ h i)),
1173	    natDegree_prod _ _ (fun i _ ↦ h i), natDegree_finSuccEquiv, degreeOf_C]
1174	  simpa only [natDegree_pow, zero_add, natDegree_X, mul_one, Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_mul,
1175	    add_right_inj] using Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ ↦ by
1176	    rw [add_comm (Polynomial.C _), natDegree_X_pow_add_C, mul_comm])
1177	
1178	private lemma degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne (hv : v ∈ f.support) (hw : w ∈ f.support) (ne : v ≠ w) :
1179	    (T f <| monomial v <| coeff v f).degreeOf 0 ≠
1180	    (T f <| monomial w <| coeff w f).degreeOf 0 := by
1181	  rw [degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hv, degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hw]
1182	  refine sum_r_mul_ne f v w (fun i ↦ ?_) (fun i ↦ ?_) ne <;>
1183	  exact lt_of_le_of_lt ((monomial_le_degreeOf i ‹_›).trans (degreeOf_le_totalDegree f i)) (by lia)
1184	
1185	private lemma leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t :
1186	    (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) ((monomial v) (coeff v f)))).leadingCoeff =
1187	    algebraMap k _ (coeff v f) := by
1188	  rw [monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_fintype]
1189	  · simp only [map_mul, map_prod, leadingCoeff_mul, leadingCoeff_prod]
1190	    rw [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, algHom_C, algebraMap_eq, finSuccEquiv_apply, eval₂Hom_C, coe_comp]
1191	    simp only [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, Function.comp_apply, leadingCoeff_C, map_pow,
1192	      leadingCoeff_pow, algebraMap_eq]
1193	    have : ∀ j, ((finSuccEquiv k n) ((T1 f) 1 (X j))).leadingCoeff = 1 := fun j ↦ by
1194	      by_cases h : j = 0
1195	      · simp [h, finSuccEquiv_apply]
1196	      · simp only [aeval_eq_bind₁, bind₁_X_right, if_neg h, one_smul, map_add, map_pow]
1197	        obtain ⟨i, rfl⟩ := Fin.exists_succ_eq.mpr h
1198	        simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, add_comm]
1199	    simp only [this, one_pow, Finset.prod_const_one, mul_one]
1200	  exact fun i ↦ pow_zero _
1201	
1202	/-- Over a domain, the `T`-transform of a non-zero `f` has, in `X_0`, a leading
1203	coefficient equal to `C (coeff v f)` for some `v ∈ f.support` (hence non-zero). The
1204	Mathlib original concludes `IsUnit` using the field hypothesis; here we keep the exact
1205	coefficient so the caller can invert it. -/
1206	private lemma T_leadingcoeff_eq (fne : f ≠ 0) :
1207	    ∃ v ∈ f.support,
1208	      (finSuccEquiv k n (T f f)).leadingCoeff = MvPolynomial.C (coeff v f) := by
1209	  obtain ⟨v, vin, vs⟩ := Finset.exists_max_image f.support
1210	    (fun v ↦ (T f ((monomial v) (coeff v f))).degreeOf 0) (support_nonempty.mpr fne)
1211	  set h := fun w ↦ (MvPolynomial.monomial w) (coeff w f)
1212	  simp only [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv] at vs
1213	  replace vs : ∀ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h x))).degree <
1214	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).degree := by
1215	    intro x hx
1216	    obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hx
1217	    apply degree_lt_degree <| lt_of_le_of_ne (vs x h1) ?_
1218	    simpa only [natDegree_finSuccEquiv]
1219	      using degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne f _ _ h1 vin <| ne_of_not_mem_cons h2
1220	  have coeff : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v + ∑ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, h x))).leadingCoeff =
1221	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).leadingCoeff := by
1222	    simp only [map_add, map_sum]
1223	    rw [add_comm]
1224	    apply leadingCoeff_add_of_degree_lt <| (lt_of_le_of_lt <| degree_sum_le _ _) ?_
1225	    have h2 : h v ≠ 0 := by simpa [h] using mem_support_iff.mp vin
1226	    replace h2 : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))) ≠ 0 := fun eq ↦ h2 <|
1227	      by simpa only [map_eq_zero_iff _ (AlgEquiv.injective _)] using eq
1228	    exact (Finset.sup_lt_iff <| Ne.bot_lt (fun x ↦ h2 <| degree_eq_bot.mp x)).mpr vs
1229	  refine ⟨v, vin, ?_⟩
1230	  rw [leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t] at coeff
1231	  nth_rw 2 [← f.support_sum_monomial_coeff]
1232	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_add_sum_diff_singleton_of_mem vin h, coeff, algebraMap_eq]
1233	
1234	/-- **L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables: monic in the distinguished variable**
1235	(`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a domain and `0 ≠ F ∈
1236	A[X_0,…,X_m]`, there is a triangular `A`-algebra automorphism `e` and a non-zero
1237	`g ∈ A` such that, after inverting `g`, the image of `e F` in `A_g[X_0,…,X_m]` —
1238	viewed via `finSuccEquiv` as a univariate polynomial in `X_0` — has a *unit* leading
1239	coefficient. (Variable convention: `finSuccEquiv` singles out `X_0`, not `X_m`; the
1240	choice is immaterial up to renaming.) -/
1241	theorem gf_nagata_monic_lastVar
1242	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (m : ℕ)
1243	    (F : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) (hF : F ≠ 0) :
1244	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
1245	      (e : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A),
1246	      IsUnit
1247	        (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv (Localization.Away g) m
1248	          (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (e F))).leadingCoeff := by
1249	  obtain ⟨v, vin, hlc⟩ := T_leadingcoeff_eq F hF
1250	  have hg0 : coeff v F ≠ 0 := mem_support_iff.mp vin
1251	  refine ⟨coeff v F, hg0, T F, ?_⟩
1252	  set g := coeff v F with hg
1253	  haveI : Nontrivial (Localization.Away g) := by
1254	    have hle : Submonoid.powers g ≤ nonZeroDivisors A := by
1255	      rw [Submonoid.powers_le]; exact mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg0
1256	    exact (IsLocalization.injective (Localization.Away g) hle).nontrivial
1257	  have hgunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) g) :=
1258	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit g
1259	  have hCunit : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) g)
1260	      : MvPolynomial (Fin m) (Localization.Away g)) := hgunit.map MvPolynomial.C
1261	  have hne : (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)))
1262	      (finSuccEquiv A m (T F F)).leadingCoeff ≠ 0 := by
1263	    rw [hlc, MvPolynomial.map_C]; exact hCunit.ne_zero
1264	  rw [finSuccEquiv_map_comm, leadingCoeff_map_of_leadingCoeff_ne_zero _ hne, hlc,
1265	    MvPolynomial.map_C]
1266	  exact hCunit
1267	
1268	end NagataNormalization
1269	
1270	/-- Compatibility of `finSuccEquiv` with the constant-variable inclusion
1271	`rename Fin.succ`: the composite `S = R[X_1,…,X_n] → R[X_0,…,X_n] → S[X_0]` is the
1272	coefficient inclusion `Polynomial.C`. Project-local because Mathlib only records the
1273	`optionEquivLeft` variant (`finSuccEquiv_rename_finSuccEquiv`). -/
1274	private theorem finSuccEquiv_rename_succ
1275	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) (s : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :
1276	    MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ s) = Polynomial.C s := by
1277	  induction s using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1278	  | C r => rw [MvPolynomial.rename_C, MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv_apply, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]; rfl
1279	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
1280	  | mul_X p i hp =>
1281	    rw [map_mul, map_mul, hp, MvPolynomial.rename_X, MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv_X_succ,
1282	      Polynomial.C_mul]
1283	
1284	/-- **L5b.3 — single-variable elimination engine** (shared)
1285	(`lem:gf_mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_monic`, Nitsure §4). Let `R` be a commutative
1286	ring and `p ∈ R[X_0,…,X_n]` a polynomial whose image under `finSuccEquiv` (a
1287	univariate polynomial in `X_0` over `S := R[X_1,…,X_n]`) has a *unit* leading
1288	coefficient — i.e. `p` is monic in `X_0` up to a unit. Then `R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p)` is
1289	module-finite over `S = R[X_1,…,X_n]`, the structure being via the constant
1290	inclusion `S ↪ R[X_0,…,X_n]` (`rename Fin.succ`) followed by `Ideal.Quotient.mk`.
1291	
1292	Encoded as `RingHom.Finite` of that composite ring map (which unfolds to
1293	`Module.Finite S (R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p))` with the exotic algebra structure) to avoid an
1294	in-statement `letI` whose `Module` synthesis loops. The proof rescales `finSuccEquiv p`
1295	by the inverse of its unit leading coefficient to a monic polynomial generating the
1296	same ideal, invokes `Polynomial.Monic.finite_quotient`, and transports along the
1297	`S`-algebra isomorphism induced by `finSuccEquiv`. -/
1298	theorem mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar
1299	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ)
1300	    (p : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R)
1301	    (hp : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).leadingCoeff) :
1302	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1303	        (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom :
1304	          MvPolynomial (Fin n) R →+* (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})).Finite := by
1305	  letI algI : Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) :=
1306	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1307	      (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom).toAlgebra
1308	  have hc : IsUnit ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
1309	    (hp.unit⁻¹).isUnit
1310	  have hcP :
1311	      IsUnit (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)) :=
1312	    hc.map Polynomial.C
1313	  have hmonic : (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1314	      * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).Monic := by
1315	    rw [Polynomial.Monic, Polynomial.leadingCoeff_C_mul_of_isUnit hc]
1316	    exact Units.inv_mul_of_eq hp.unit_spec
1317	  have hspan : Ideal.span {Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) :
1318	        MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}
1319	      = Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p} :=
1320	    Ideal.span_singleton_mul_left_unit hcP (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p)
1321	  have hfinstd : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1322	      (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) := by
1323	    rw [← hspan]; exact hmonic.finite_quotient
1324	  have hJ : Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}
1325	      = Ideal.map ((MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n).toRingEquiv :
1326	          MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R →+* Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R))
1327	        (Ideal.span {p}) := by
1328	    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]; rfl
1329	  let ψ : (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
1330	      ≃+* (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) :=
1331	    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {p}) (Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})
1332	      (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n).toRingEquiv hJ
1333	  have hsmul : ∀ s : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R,
1334	      ψ (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1335	          (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) s)
1336	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1337	          (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})
1338	          s := by
1339	    intro s
1340	    change ψ (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ s))
1341	      = Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (Polynomial.C s)
1342	    rw [Ideal.quotientEquiv_mk]
1343	    exact congrArg _ (finSuccEquiv_rename_succ R n s)
1344	  let Ψ : (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
1345	      ≃ₐ[MvPolynomial (Fin n) R]
1346	      (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) :=
1347	    AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv hsmul

1636	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
1637	  -- `C (algebraMap A A_g a')` is the image of the `P`-constant `C a'` under `algebraMap P Pg`.
1638	  have hCeq : ∀ a' : A,
1639	      (MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a') :
1640	        MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g))
1641	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A)
1642	          (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g)) (MvPolynomial.C a') := by
1643	    intro a'
1644	    rw [show (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A)
1645	          (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g)) (MvPolynomial.C a'))
1646	        = MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (MvPolynomial.C a') from rfl,
1647	      MvPolynomial.map_C]

1649	  -- from `P` (via `algebraMap P Pg`) and the Nagata equivalence `ebar` fixes constants from `P`.

1651	      θ (MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a'))
1652	      = Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {Fg})
1653	          (MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a')) := by
1654	    intro a'
1655	    have hebarfix : ebar (MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a'))
1656	        = MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a') := by

1658	      congr 1
1659	      exact he_C a'

1661	    have hρC : ρ (MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a'))
1662	        = Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {G})
1663	            (MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a')) := by
1664	      rw [hρ_def]; simp
1665	    rw [hρC, Ideal.quotientEquiv_symm_mk]
1666	    congr 1
1667	    exact (RingEquiv.symm_apply_eq ebar).mpr hebarfix.symm
1668	  -- Hence the `A`-action through the `A_g`-action factors as the original `A`-action, giving

1678	  letI eAgL : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T

1680	    LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers g) (Localization.Away g)
1681	      (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv (Submonoid.powers g)
1682	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers g) T)
1683	        ((LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap MC T).restrictScalars A))
1684	  letI eAdd : LocalizedModule MC T ≃+ LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T :=
1685	    eAgL.symm.toAddEquiv

1687	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) :=
1688	    pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := MvPolynomial (Fin m) (Localization.Away g)) eAdd
1689	  -- The pulled-back `R`-action restricted to `A_g` agrees with the canonical `A_g`-action on
1690	  -- `T_g`, because `eAgL` is `A_g`-linear; this gives the required scalar tower.
1691	  haveI htower : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
1692	      (MvPolynomial (Fin m) (Localization.Away g)) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) := by
1693	    refine IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun a x => ?_
1694	    change eAgL.symm (a • eAgL x) = a • x
1695	    rw [eAgL.symm.map_smul, eAgL.symm_apply_apply]

1697	    finite_of_pullbackModuleAddEquiv eAdd⟩

1700	-- The doubly-localised carrier `LocalizedModule (powers h) (LocalizedModule (powers g) T)`
1701	-- makes instance search for the transported `Localization.Away (g*a)`-action explore many
1702	-- `OreLocalization`/`LocalizedModule` paths; raise the synthesis budget accordingly.
1703	/-- **Descent of generic freeness across a tower of `Away` localisations.** If after
1704	inverting `g ∈ A` and then `h ∈ A_g = Localization.Away g` the `A`-module `T` becomes
1705	free, then it becomes free after inverting a single non-zero `f ∈ A`.
1706	
1707	This is the "descend the witness from `A_g` to `A`" step (step 4) of the polynomial-ring
1708	core `exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial`: the inductive hypothesis is applied at the
1709	new base `A_g` and produces freeness of the *iterated* localisation
1710	`(T_g)_h := LocalizedModule (powers h) (LocalizedModule (powers g) T)` over `(A_g)_h`. Here
1711	`h = a / gᵏ`, so `(A_g)_h ≅ A_{g·a}` (`IsLocalization.Away.mul_of_associated`) and the
1712	iterated localisation is the single localisation of `T` at `powers (g·a)`; transporting
1713	freeness across the ring iso (`Module.Free.of_ringEquiv`) and the localised-module
1714	identification gives `T_f` free over `A_f` with `f := g·a`. -/
1715	theorem free_localizationAway_of_away_tower
1716	    (A T : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [AddCommGroup T] [Module A T]
1717	    {g : A} (hg : g ≠ 0) {h : Localization.Away g} (hh : h ≠ 0)
1718	    (hfree : Module.Free (Localization.Away h)
1719	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))) :
1720	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
1721	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) T) := by
1722	  classical
1723	  -- Witness `f := g · a`, where `a ∈ A` is a numerator of `h` over `A_g`.
1724	  -- === Ring side: clear the denominator of `h` ===
1725	  obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (Submonoid.powers g) h
1726	  -- `hs : h * algebraMap A A_g ↑s = algebraMap A A_g a`, with `s ∈ powers g` a unit denominator.
1727	  have hsunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) (s : A)) :=
1728	    IsLocalization.map_units (Localization.Away g) s
1729	  -- `algebraMap ↑s` is the unit `u`; record `algebraMap ↑s * u⁻¹ = 1`.
1730	  have hsu : algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) (s : A) * ↑(hsunit.unit⁻¹) = 1 := by
1731	    have h1 := hsunit.unit.mul_inv
1732	    rwa [hsunit.unit_spec] at h1
1733	  -- `a ≠ 0`: else `h * algebraMap ↑s = 0`, and `algebraMap ↑s` is a unit, forcing `h = 0`.
1734	  have ha : a ≠ 0 := by
1735	    rintro rfl
1736	    rw [map_zero] at hs
1737	    apply hh
1738	    calc h = h * (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) (s : A) * ↑(hsunit.unit⁻¹)) := by
1739	            rw [hsu, mul_one]
1740	      _ = h * algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) (s : A) * ↑(hsunit.unit⁻¹) := by rw [mul_assoc]
1741	      _ = 0 := by rw [hs, zero_mul]
1742	  have hf0 : g * a ≠ 0 := mul_ne_zero hg ha
1743	  -- `algebraMap a` and `h` are associated (differ by the unit `algebraMap ↑s`).
1744	  have hassoc : Associated (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a) h := by
1745	    refine ⟨hsunit.unit⁻¹, ?_⟩
1746	    rw [← hs, mul_assoc, hsu, mul_one]
1747	  -- The canonical tower `A → A_g → A_h` already holds; `A_h` is then the localisation of `A`
1748	  -- away from `g · a` (`mul_of_associated`).
1749	  haveI hlocAh : IsLocalization.Away (g * a) (Localization.Away h) :=
1750	    (IsLocalization.Away.mul_of_associated g a h hassoc :
1751	      IsLocalization.Away (g * a) (Localization.Away h))
1752	  -- === Module side: the composite localisation map and its `IsLocalizedModule` structure ===
1753	  -- `ψ : T →ₗ[A] D` factoring `T → T_g → D` (restrict the second map to `A`).
1754	  let ψ : T →ₗ[A]
1755	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) :=
1756	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers h)
1757	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)).restrictScalars A ∘ₗ
1758	      LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers g) T
1759	  -- The `A`-action on `D` factors through `A_h`.
1760	  haveI towerAAhD : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away h)
1761	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)) := by
1762	    refine IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun a' x => ?_
1763	    rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply A (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away h) a',
1764	      IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (Localization.Away h),
1765	      IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (Localization.Away g)]
1766	  -- Localisation-of-localisation as a base change: `D` is the base change of `T` to `A_h`.
1767	  have hbcψ : IsBaseChange (Localization.Away h) ψ :=
1768	    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers g) (Localization.Away g)
1769	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers g) T)).comp
1770	      (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers h) (Localization.Away h)
1771	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers h)
1772	          (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)))
1773	  haveI hLMψ : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) ψ :=
1774	    (isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) (Localization.Away h) ψ).mpr hbcψ
1775	  -- === Transport freeness from `A_h` down to `A_{g·a}` ===
1776	  refine ⟨g * a, hf0, ?_⟩
1777	  -- `A_{g·a} ≃ₐ[A] A_h` (two localisations of `A` away from `g · a`).
1778	  let σ : Localization.Away (g * a) ≃ₐ[A] Localization.Away h :=
1779	    IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) (Localization.Away (g * a))
1780	      (Localization.Away h)
1781	  -- The `A`-linear uniqueness iso between the two localisations of `T` at `powers (g·a)`.
1782	  let ε : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) T ≃ₗ[A]
1783	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) :=
1784	    IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv (Submonoid.powers (g * a))
1785	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) T) ψ
1786	  -- Equip `D` with the `A_{g·a}`-action transported through `σ` (acting via `σ`).
1787	  letI modLga : Module (Localization.Away (g * a))
1788	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)) :=
1789	    Module.compHom
1790	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
1791	      (σ.toAlgHom.toRingHom : Localization.Away (g * a) →+* Localization.Away h)
1792	  -- The `A`-action through this new `A_{g·a}`-action is the original one.
1793	  haveI towerLga : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away (g * a))
1794	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)) := by
1795	    refine IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun a' x => ?_
1796	    change σ (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (g * a)) a') • x = a' • x
1797	    rw [AlgEquiv.commutes]
1798	    exact IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (Localization.Away h) a' x
1799	  -- `D` is free over `A_{g·a}`: transport a basis of `D` over `A_h` through the ring iso `σ.symm`.
1800	  haveI : Module.Free (Localization.Away h)
1801	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)) := hfree
1802	  have hcompat : ∀ (c : Localization.Away h)
1803	      (x : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)),
1804	      (σ.symm.toRingEquiv c) • x = c • x := fun c x => by
1805	    change σ (σ.symm c) • x = c • x
1806	    rw [AlgEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
1807	  have hDfree : Module.Free (Localization.Away (g * a))
1808	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)) :=
1809	    Module.Free.of_basis
1810	      ((Module.Free.chooseBasis (Localization.Away h)
1811	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h)
1812	          (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))).mapCoeffs σ.symm.toRingEquiv hcompat)
1813	  -- The `A`-linear `ε` upgrades to `A_{g·a}`-linear; conclude freeness of `T_{g·a}`.
1814	  have εL : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) T ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (g * a)]
1815	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) :=
1816	    LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers (g * a))
1817	      (Localization.Away (g * a)) ε
1818	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv' hDfree εL.symm

1820	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
1821	-- The reindexed localised quotient `(N ⧸ range φ)_g` carries stacked
1822	-- `OreLocalization`/`MvPolynomial` module structures whose instance search is expensive;
1823	-- raise the synthesis budget for the inductive-step wiring.
1824	/-- **L5 — polynomial-ring core of generic freeness** (`lem:gf_polynomial_core`,
1825	Nitsure §4, the genuine Mathlib-absent residue). For `A` a noetherian domain and
1826	`d ≥ 0`, a finite module `N` over the polynomial ring `A[X_1,…,X_d]`, regarded as
1827	an `A`-module via the scalar tower `A → A[X] → N`, becomes free after inverting a
1828	single non-zero `f ∈ A`.
1829	
1830	This is the bottom of the Nitsure §4 induction. The base case `d = 0` is the
1831	finite-`A`-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`
1832	(`MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`). The inductive step builds the generic-rank short
1833	exact sequence `0 → A_g[X]^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0` with `T` torsion of support
1834	dimension `< d`, applies the inductive hypothesis to `T` and the torsion base
1835	case `exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion`, and splices via
1836	`exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact`. -/
1837	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial
1838	    (A : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1839	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type u) [AddCommGroup N]
1840	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
1841	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
1842	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
1843	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N) := by
1844	  -- The genuine proof is a *strong* induction on `d`, with the module `N` and its
1845	  -- instances universally quantified in the motive: the inductive hypothesis must
1846	  -- apply to the lower-support-dimension torsion quotient `T` (a module over a
1847	  -- polynomial ring in fewer variables `m < d`), and the module instances depend
1848	  -- on `d`, so a faithful induction must re-quantify `N` over each `d`. The
1849	  -- `generalizing N` reverts `N` together with all five of its `d`-dependent
1850	  -- instances, so the IH has the shape
1851	  --   IH : ∀ m < d, ∀ (N : Type _) [AddCommGroup N] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N]
1852	  --          [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N] [Module A N]
1853	  --          [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N],
1854	  --          ∃ f ≠ 0, Module.Free (A_f) (N_f).
1855	  induction d using Nat.strong_induction_on generalizing A N with
1856	  | _ d IH =>
1857	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos d with hd | hd
1858	    · -- Base case `d = 0`: `MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`, so `N` is module-finite
1859	      -- over `A` itself (via `Module.Finite.trans` through the iso), and the claim
1860	      -- is the finite-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`.
1861	      subst hd
1862	      haveI : Module.Finite A (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) :=
1863	        Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv A (Fin 0)).symm.toLinearEquiv
1864	      haveI : Module.Finite A N := Module.Finite.trans (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) N
1865	      exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A N
1866	    · -- Inductive step. Pass to `K = Frac A` and split on whether `N_K = 0`.
1867	      by_cases htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) N)
1868	      · -- Torsion sub-case `N_K = 0`: closed by the L1 torsion base case applied
1869	        -- with the finite-type polynomial ring `B := A[X_1,…,X_d]` as the module's
1870	        -- ring of definition.
1871	        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N htors
1872	      · -- Generic-rank dévissage (the genuine generic-rank residue): with `N_K ≠ 0`,
1873	        -- let `m` be the generic rank of `N` over the domain `A[X_1,…,X_d]`; choosing
1874	        -- `m` elements whose images form a `K(X)`-basis and clearing denominators
1875	        -- gives, after inverting some `g ≠ 0`, a short exact sequence
1876	        --   `0 → A_g[X]^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0`
1877	        -- with `T` torsion of support dimension `< d`. After a Noether-normalisation
1878	        -- reindex `T` is finite over a polynomial ring in `m' < d` variables, so the
1879	        -- IH applies:  `IH m' (by …) T` yields `h ≠ 0` with `T_h` free over `A_h`.
1880	        -- The free middle term `A_g[X]^{⊕m}` localises to a free `A_{gh}`-module by
1881	        -- the `d = 0` leaf (`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`) applied
1882	        -- coordinatewise, and `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3)
1883	        -- splices the localised SES to give `N_f` free over `A_f` with `f := g·h`.
1884	        --
1885	        -- The IH is now genuinely in scope and quantifies over the base domain `A`
1886	        -- (the structural fix of this iter): `IH m' (hm' : m' < d) A_g T_g` typechecks
1887	        -- at the reindexed base `A_g = Localization.Away g`.
1888	        --
1889	        -- The generic-rank SES `0 → (Fin m → P) → N → T → 0` (`gf_generic_rank_ses`),
1890	        -- with `T := N ⧸ range φ` torsion over `P := A[X_1,…,X_d]`.
1891	        obtain ⟨m, φ, hφinj, hTtors⟩ := gf_generic_rank_ses A d N
1892	        -- Step 1: the torsion cokernel `N ⧸ range φ` is a finite `P`-module (its restricted
1893	        -- `A`-module structure + scalar tower `A → P → quotient` are found by instances).
1894	        haveI hTfin : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) :=
1895	          Module.Finite.of_surjective (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ_surjective
1896	        -- Step 2: reindex the torsion cokernel onto `m' < d` variables over `A_g`.
1897	        -- `gf_torsion_reindex` now emits its `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g`-action (`hmod1`) and the
1898	        -- scalar tower (`htower`) over the *canonical* (`inferInstance`) `Module A_g`-action on the
1899	        -- localised module — the redundant `Module A_g T_g` existential was dropped, so the
1900	        -- `A_g`-action the IH and `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower` synthesise is exactly the
1901	        -- one `htower` refers to.  This dissolves the former `OreLocalization`
1902	        -- instance-presentation diamond: there is now a single `Module A_g T_g` instance in play.
1903	        obtain ⟨g, hg0, m', hm'lt, hmod1, htower, hfin⟩ :=
1904	          gf_torsion_reindex A d hd (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) hTtors
1905	        -- Step 3: the inductive hypothesis at the new noetherian-domain base `A_g`.
1906	        haveI hdomg : IsDomain (Localization.Away g) :=
1907	          IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
1908	            (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr (mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg0))
1909	        haveI hnoethg : IsNoetherianRing (Localization.Away g) := inferInstance
1910	        -- Register the reindex's `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g`-module structure, its finiteness, and
1911	        -- its scalar tower in the local instance cache so the IH application infers them.
1912	        letI := hmod1
1913	        haveI := hfin
1914	        haveI := htower
1915	        -- Steps 3–4: the IH at base `A_g` produces `hh ≠ 0` in `A_g` with the doubly-localised
1916	        -- `(T_g)_hh` free over `(A_g)_hh`; the tower-descent helper
1917	        -- `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower` descends that witness to a single `f := g·a ∈ A`.
1918	        obtain ⟨f, hf0, hTf_free⟩ :
1919	            ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
1920	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ)) := by
1921	          obtain ⟨hh, hh0, hfree_Tgh⟩ :=
1922	            IH m' hm'lt (Localization.Away g)
1923	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ))
1924	          exact free_localizationAway_of_away_tower A (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) hg0 hh0 hfree_Tgh
1925	        -- Step 5: splice the localised SES.  The free left end `Fin m → P` (a finite power
1926	        -- of the `A`-free polynomial ring `P`) localises to a free `A_f`-module by base
1927	        -- change, and `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3) splices.
1928	        haveI hM'free_base : Module.Free A (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) := inferInstance
1929	        have hM'_free : Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
1930	            (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)) :=
1931	          (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
1932	            (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f)
1933	              (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A))).free
1934	        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
1935	          (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) φ
1936	          (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ hφinj (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ_surjective
1937	          (by rw [LinearMap.exact_iff, Submodule.ker_mkQ]) hf0 hf0 hM'_free hTf_free
